title: No Dumb Questions: What is cloud computing and why is everyone doing it?

title: Building a general-purpose accessibility agent—and what we learned in the process
summary: It is an understatement to say agents have become a popular way of working with code. GitHub has adopted agent-based code creation and editing for many of its initiatives, including piloting an agent to help with our commitment to accessibility .
timeline_entries: Providing engineers with reliable, just-in-time answers to accessibility questions in the GitHub Copilot CLI and the Copilot VS Code integration.
To date, the agent has reviewed 3,535 pull requests, with a 68% resolution rate . In order of occurrence, the top five issue types center around:
Interested? We ll be outlining successes and lessons learned with this experiment, with the hopes that it can help with other teams accessibility journeys.

The European Accessibility Act is now in effect. Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act is set to establish meeting WCAG 2.1 AA as the legal definition of done in April of 2027. LLM agents can read and take acti

To say it plainly: Organizations will be at a disadvantage if they have not already invested in manually identifying and remediating accessibility issues . There are many reasons for this, including building an accessibi
Crosslinks to the Pull Request that addressed the issue

Because of this, we instructed the agent to investigate these issues to see if there are related code and language snippets it can extrapolate from. This is one area where the non-deterministic fuzzy matching behavior of
Much like with any other specialized domain area, vague instructions in a skill file won t cut it. Telling an LLM to use accessibility best practices with a short list of examples won t work well.

sections.overview:
It is an understatement to say agents have become a popular way of working with code. GitHub has adopted agent-based code creation and editing for many of its initiatives, including piloting an agent to help with our commitment to accessibility . GitHub is currently piloting an experimental general-purpose accessibility agent to achieve two main goals:

 
 Providing engineers with reliable, just-in-time answers to accessibility questions in the GitHub Copilot CLI and the Copilot VS Code integration.
sections.technical_breakdown:
In order of occurrence, the top five issue types center around:

 
 Making structure and relationships clear to assistive technologies 

 Providing clear and concise names for interactive controls 

 Ensuring users are aware of important announcements 

 Ensuring there are text alternatives for non-text content 

 Moving keyboard focus through pages and views in a logical order 

 

 Each of these issue types represents friction and barriers automatically removed that would have otherwise inhibited use of GitHub for people who use and rely on assistive technology.

If you re not familiar, you can learn more about LLMs on our blog .

To that point, GitHub has a mature system in place for logging accessibility issues, as well as verifying fixes to issues are working as intended.

This includes:

 
 A structured template for reporting problems

 Steps to reproduce the issue

 A rich layer of metadata about the issue s severity level, service area, and applicable WCAG success criterion 

 Crosslinks to the Pull Request that addressed the issue

 Acceptance criteria

 

 In addition, all the issues are centralized to a single repository.

While this issue-logging effort predated the explosion in popularity of LLM tooling, its highly consistent and structured nature made it an ideal corpus of content for the accessibility agent to reference.
sections.impact:
In order of occurrence, the top five issue types center around:

 
 Making structure and relationships clear to assistive technologies 

 Providing clear and concise names for interactive controls 

 Ensuring users are aware of important announcements 

 Ensuring there are text alternatives for non-text content 

 Moving keyboard focus through pages and views in a logical order 

 

 Each of these issue types represents friction and barriers automatically removed that would have otherwise inhibited use of GitHub for people who use and rely on assistive technology. We are instead attempting to augment our peers efforts , to better help them remove the barriers that may be created as a result of how we construct GitHub s user interfaces. Understanding, honoring, and socializing this better helps set the agent s scope of responsibility. Efficient token consumption 

 Accessibility is a holistic concern , intersecting with code, design, copywriting, and numerous other disciplines involved with creating user interfaces.

Classified impact band: medium. Affected layer signal: cloud — aws.
sections.resolution:
To that point, GitHub has a mature system in place for logging accessibility issues, as well as verifying fixes to issues are working as intended.

We found that compelling the agent to execute its sub-agent instructions in a fixed order was key.

Implementer template schema: This focuses on what to fix and how to fix it.
sections.lessons_learned:
We ll be outlining successes and lessons learned with this experiment, with the hopes that it can help with other teams accessibility journeys.

Improved when its output is periodically reviewed to identify areas it needs better instruction.

Some specific posts you may find helpful are:

 
 A guide to deciding what AI model to use in GitHub Copilot 

 GitHub for Beginners: How to get LLMs to do what you want 

 How to write a great agents.md: Lessons from over 2,500 repositories 

 Multi-agent workflows often fail.

We are instead attempting to augment our peers efforts , to better help them remove the barriers that may be created as a result of how we construct GitHub s user interfaces.

Crosslinks to the Pull Request that addressed the issue
Because of this, we instructed the agent to investigate these issues to see if there are related code and language snippets it can extrapolate from. This is one area where the non-deterministic fuzzy matching behavior of
Much like with any other specialized domain area, vague instructions in a skill file won t cut it. Telling an LLM to use accessibility best practices with a short list of examples won t work well.
sections.trends:
Large-scale outages remain the forcing function for resilient architecture, communication, and automation investments.

Public postmortems compress organizational learning into patterns the broader industry can reuse.

Observability shifts remain tightly coupled with incident response quality and capacity planning accuracy.

title: Raising the bar: Quality, shared responsibility, and the future of GitHub’s bug bounty program
summary: The security research community is one of GitHub s greatest assets. Every year, researchers from around the world help us find and fix vulnerabilities, making the platform safer for over 180 million developers. Our bug bounty program exists because we believe that collaboration with external researchers is one of the most effective ways to improve security, and we remain deeply committed to it.
timeline_entries: The security research community is one of GitHub s greatest assets. Every year, researchers from around the world help us find and fix vulnerabilities, making the platform safer for over 180 million developers. Our bug b
We don t want to go that direction. Instead, we want to invest in making our program better.

We want to be explicit about this: we have no problem with researchers using AI tools. AI is a force multiplier, and we expect it to play an increasing role in security research. We use AI across our own internal securit

We d also ask researchers to keep reports concise and structured. A strong report has three things: a short summary of the issue, clear steps to reproduce with supporting evidence (screenshots, HTTP requests, terminal ou

One pattern we see frequently deserves its own discussion. Many reports describe scenarios where a user interacts with attacker-controlled content (a malicious repository, a crafted issue, untrusted code) and experiences

We invest heavily in systems and teams dedicated to detecting and handling malicious content across the platform, from automated scanning to manual review. That said, GitHub operates on a shared responsibility model . Us

If you re newer to bug bounty , welcome! Take a few minutes to read our scope, review the ineligible list, and invest in a working proof of concept before submitting. Quality submissions from new researchers are always v

Going forward, we re updating how we handle these cases. Submissions that don t demonstrate significant security impact but do result in a code or documentation fix will be recognized with GitHub swag rather than a bount

sections.overview: The security research community is one of GitHub s greatest assets. Every year, researchers from around the world help us find and fix vulnerabilities, making the platform safer for over 180 million developers. Our bug bounty program exists because we believe that collaboration with external researchers is one of the most effective ways to improve security, and we remain deeply committed to it.
sections.technical_breakdown:
A strong report has three things: a short summary of the issue, clear steps to reproduce with supporting evidence (screenshots, HTTP requests, terminal output), and an impact statement explaining what an attacker can actually achieve.

Verbose reports such as multi-page theoretical narratives, restated background context, or AI-generated filler slow down triage because the actual finding gets buried.

Nothing changes for you except faster response times as we reduce queue noise.

This lets us acknowledge the contribution while focusing our bounty resources on the findings that have the greatest impact on platform security.

The post Raising the bar: Quality, shared responsibility, and the future of GitHub s bug bounty program appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
sections.impact:
Reports covering known ineligible categories (DMARC/SPF/DKIM configuration, user enumeration, missing security headers without a demonstrated attack path, and others) will be closed as Not Applicable, which may impact your HackerOne Signal and reputation. Take a few minutes to read our scope, review the ineligible list, and invest in a working proof of concept before submitting. However, alongside the growth in legitimate reports, we ve seen a sharp increase in submissions that don t demonstrate real security impact. Going forward, reports will be evaluated more strictly against these criteria:

 
 A working proof of concept with demonstrated security impact.

Classified impact band: medium. Affected layer signal: general production.
sections.resolution:
Every year, researchers from around the world help us find and fix vulnerabilities, making the platform safer for over 180 million developers.

Submissions that don t demonstrate significant security impact but do result in a code or documentation fix will be recognized with GitHub swag rather than a bounty payout.
sections.lessons_learned:
Our bug bounty program exists because we believe that collaboration with external researchers is one of the most effective ways to improve security, and we remain deeply committed to it.

Instead, we want to invest in making our program better.

Going forward, reports will be evaluated more strictly against these criteria:

 
 A working proof of concept with demonstrated security impact.

Some reports identify hardening opportunities or documentation gaps that, while not exploitable, still lead to improvements we choose to make.

title: Kubernetes v1.36: Mixed Version Proxy Graduates to Beta
summary: Back in Kubernetes 1.28, we introduced the Mixed Version Proxy (MVP) as an Alpha feature (under the feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy ) in a previous blog post . The goal was simple but critical: make cluster upgrades safer by ensuring that requests for resources not yet known to an older API server are correctly routed to a newer peer API server, instead of returning an incorrect...
timeline_entries: Back in Kubernetes 1.28, we introduced the Mixed Version Proxy (MVP) as an Alpha feature (under the feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy ) in a previous blog post . The goal was simple but critical: make clus

Without MVP, if a client request lands on an API server that does not serve the requested resource (e.g., a new API version introduced in the upgrade), that server returns a 404 Not Found . This is technically incorrect
MVP solves this by proxying the request to a peer API server that can serve it.
Client->>API_Server_A: 1. Request for Resource (e.g., v2)
API_Server_A->>API_Server_B: 3. Proxies request (adds x-kubernetes-peer-proxied header)
API_Server_B->>API_Server_B: 4. Processes request locally
The 1.28 blog post noted a significant gap: while we could proxy resource requests, discovery requests still only showed what the local API server knew about.
Client->>API_Server_A: 1. Request Discovery Document

sections.overview: Back in Kubernetes 1.28, we introduced the Mixed Version Proxy (MVP) as an Alpha feature (under the feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy ) in a previous blog post . The goal was simple but critical: make cluster upgrades safer by ensuring that requests for resources not yet known to an older API server are correctly routed to a newer peer API server, instead of returning an incorrect 404 Not Found . We are excited to announce that the Mixed Version Proxy is moving to Beta in Kubernetes 1.36 and will be enabled by default!
sections.technical_breakdown:
Back in Kubernetes 1.28, we introduced the Mixed Version Proxy (MVP) as an Alpha feature (under the feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy ) in a previous blog post .

Without this, proxying will fail due to TLS verification errors.

 --peer-advertise-ip and --peer-advertise-port : These flags are used to set the network address that peers should use to reach this API server.

If you have complex network topologies where API servers communicate over a specific internal interface, setting these flags explicitly is highly recommended.

We are excited to announce that the Mixed Version Proxy is moving to Beta in Kubernetes 1.36 and will be enabled by default!

In a highly available control plane undergoing an upgrade, you often have API servers running different versions.
sections.impact:
Without MVP, if a client request lands on an API server that does not serve the requested resource (e.g., a new API version introduced in the upgrade), that server returns a 404 Not Found . MVP solves this by proxying the request to a peer API server that can serve it.

 
 
sequenceDiagram
participant Client
participant API_Server_A as API Server A (Older/Different)
participant API_Server_B as API Server B (Newer/Capable)
Client->>API_Server_A: 1. Returns Response
API_Server_A-->>Client: 6. Now, when a client performs discovery (e.g., listing available APIs), the API server merges its local view with the discovery data from all active peers.

Classified impact band: medium. Affected layer signal: platform — kubernetes.
sections.resolution: Resolution detail may be partial in the RSS excerpt. Refer to the original write-up for timelines, mitigations, and verification steps.
sections.lessons_learned:
Configuring with kubeadm 
 If you manage your cluster with kubeadm , you can configure these flags in your ClusterConfiguration file:

 apiVersion : kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta4 
 kind : ClusterConfiguration 
 apiServer : 
 extraArgs : 
 peer-ca-file : "/etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt" 
 # peer-advertise-ip and port if needed 
 Call to action 
 If you are running multi-master clusters and upgrading them regularly, the Mixed Version Proxy is a major safety improvement.
sections.root_cause_highlight:
Likely root-cause theme: control plane issue. In a highly available control plane undergoing an upgrade, you often have API servers running different versions. This is technically incorrect because the resource is available in the cluster, just not on that specific server. Without this, proxying will fail due to TLS verification errors.

 --peer-advertise-ip and --peer-advertise-port : These flags are used to set the network address that peers should use to reach this API server.

MVP solves this by proxying the request to a peer API server that can serve it.
Client->>API_Server_A: 1. Request for Resource (e.g., v2)
API_Server_A->>API_Server_B: 3. Proxies request (adds x-kubernetes-peer-proxied header)
API_Server_B->>API_Server_B: 4. Processes request locally
The 1.28 blog post noted a significant gap: while we could proxy resource requests, discovery requests still only showed what the local API server knew about.

email_summary: Back in Kubernetes 1.28, we introduced the Mixed Version Proxy (MVP) as an Alpha feature (under the feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy ) in a previous blog post . The goal was simple but critical: make cluster upgrades safer by ensuring that requests for resources not yet known to an older API server are correctly routed to a newer peer API server, instead of returning an incorrect 404 Not Found .

title: Observability and human intuition in an AI world
summary: In this two for one episode recorded at HumanX, Ryan is first joined by Christine Yen, CEO of Honeycomb, to discuss how AI compresses the software development lifecycle, making observability about capturing the right telemetry. Then, Spiros Xanthos, founder and CEO of Resolve AI, shares with us how AI coding increases code volume but decreases human intuition, making production operations harder...

sections.overview: In this two for one episode recorded at HumanX, Ryan is first joined by Christine Yen, CEO of Honeycomb, to discuss how AI compresses the software development lifecycle, making observability about capturing the right telemetry. Then, Spiros Xanthos, founder and CEO of Resolve AI, shares with us how AI coding increases code volume but decreases human intuition, making production operations harder than ever.

email_summary: In this two for one episode recorded at HumanX, Ryan is first joined by Christine Yen, CEO of Honeycomb, to discuss how AI compresses the software development lifecycle, making observability about capturing the right telemetry. Then, Spiros Xanthos, founder and CEO of Resolve AI, shares with us how AI coding increases code volume but decreases human intuition, making production operations harder than ever.
